article_title: IoT Security Regulation Accelerates on Both Sides of the Atlantic as NIST Rewrites Federal Guidance and the EU CRA's First Deadline Approaches

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: IoT Security Regulation Accelerates on Both Sides of the Atlantic as NIST Rewrites Federal Guidance and the EU CRA's First Deadline Approaches
content_preview.summary: NIST is overhauling its IoT cybersecurity guidance for federal agencies while the EU Cyber Resilience Act's first enforcement deadline in September 2026 forces manufacturers to build vulnerability reporting infrastructure from scratch.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The regulatory landscape for Internet of Things security is shifting rapidly as two of the world's largest markets move to impose binding cybersecurity requirements on connected devices. In the United States, the National Institute of Standards and Technology held a two-day workshop at the end of March to gather stakeholder input on a planned overhaul of its federal IoT cybersecurity guidance. editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive IoT security regulation article covering both US and EU regulatory frameworks with 7 sources.
editor_notes.source_verification: NIST blog confirms March 31-April 1 workshop, SP 800-213 revision. EU digital strategy confirms CRA September 11 2026 reporting deadline, December 2027 full obligations. Keysight 403 but URL is legitimate. All major regulatory facts verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: NIST workshop dates and SP 800-213 focus confirmed. CRA September 2026 deadline and manufacturer requirements confirmed via official EU source. Fine amounts (€15M/2.5%) not in the official page summary but are in the regulation text.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Good balance of US and EU regulatory landscapes.
editor_notes.originality: No prior IoT security regulation coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong cybersecurity policy article. Human-requested with heightened scrutiny. Approved.
